card.intuition: A service that converts long URLs into short, unique aliases, involving high read throughput, unique ID generation, and efficient redirection logic.
card.analogy: A coat check at a theater: you give them your big, bulky coat (Long URL), and they give you a tiny plastic token (Short URL). When you leave, you hand back the token, and they give you your coat back immediately.
when_to_use: Building services like bit.ly or tinyurl.com
Sharing links on platforms with character limits (like SMS or X)
Tracking click analytics for marketing campaigns
gotchas: Collision risk: if you use a random 6-character string, you will eventually have two different URLs with the same short code. Base-62 encoding of a unique ID is safer.
Read vs Write ratio: URL shorteners are almost always 100:1 read-heavy, so heavy caching (Redis) is mandatory.

exercise.prompt: Estimate the storage required for 100 million URLs per month over 5 years. Assume 500 bytes per record.
exercise.starter_code:
monthly_urls = 100_000_000
years = 5
bytes_per_record = 500

total_storage_gb = (monthly_urls * 12 * years * bytes_per_record) / (1024**3)
# Result is approximately ______ GB
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return 2700 < int(ans) < 2900
assert check("2794")
print("PASS")
